workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days?
Are there recurring email types I write regularly like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests?
Which emails required the most back-and-forth this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat - where I do the same sequence of actions?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Customer Escalation Eval Quality Thread Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: Evals / Customer Escalations / Quality Discussions: 12-18 emails, replies/forwards in long threads. Reading 45-60 min, replying/synthesizing 30-45 min. Approx 1.25-1.75 hours total.

signals.title: ADO Work Item Notification Batch Review
signals.rawExcerpt: Azure DevOps Auto-generated Work Item Notifications: 25-35 emails (largest by count). Most are skimmed or ignored. Approx 10-15 min total. Prime candidate for rules or digesting.

signals.title: Security and Compliance Alert Handling
signals.rawExcerpt: Security / Compliance Alerts (Purview, File Blocked): 15-20 emails, many near-duplicates. Initial attention spike 5-10 min. Approx 10-15 min total.

signals.title: EvalCon Strategic Participation Planning
signals.rawExcerpt: EvalCon / Visibility / Strategic Alignment Threads: 4-6 emails, mostly forwards. Reading/scanning 10-15 min, thinking about positioning 10 min. Approx 20-25 min total.

signals.title: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invite Sending
signals.rawExcerpt: Loop / Workspace Invites: Automated or semi-automated invitations to collaborate in a workspace. Indicate a recurring collaboration workflow initiated by the PM.

signals.title: Customer-Donated Evals Playbook Back-and-Forth
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread: Re: Customer donated evals added Playbook - sustained replies across multiple days and stakeholders. Cross-system discussion (CLS CRM vs SuccessHub). Core topic: how customer-donated evals should be incorporated into standard pitch decks.

signals.title: Urgent Declarative Agent Eval Escalation Response
signals.rawExcerpt: RE: URGENT: DBS: Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites - Large distribution list, urgent flag, rapid back-and-forth troubleshooting. Blockers and tooling gaps when running evals for a large-scale declarative agent.

signals.title: Eval vs Real Copilot UX Quality Discrepancy Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Fw: Barclays - Evals vs M365 Copilot quality issue - Active escalation with Sev-3 incidents. Customer trust issues caused by inconsistent responses between Eval/Test Pane results and real M365 Copilot UX, ahead of a high-stakes GA timeline.

signals.title: Inbound-to-Meeting-to-Document Workflow Loop
signals.rawExcerpt: Inbound -> Coordinate -> Artifact pattern: Receive email or Teams message, schedule a meeting to align or unblock, then create or update a doc/deck/Loop page with outcomes. Classic coordination-heavy PM loop and prime candidate for partial automation.

signals.title: Weekly Friday Show Broadcast All-Hands
signals.rawExcerpt: The Friday Show - Cadence: Weekly (recurring). Attended ~28 minutes this past week. Three recurring agenda themes: product deep dives, customer stories, and community moments. ~90+ invitees. Large-scale sync/broadcast rather than a working session.

signals.title: 1:1 Manager Check-In (Twice Weekly)
signals.rawExcerpt: 1:1 PM and Manager - When: Mondays and Thursdays, 2:35-3:00 PM. PM and Manager only. Fixed cadence, identical participants, narrow scope. Qualifies as a true weekly anchor meeting.

signals.title: Weekly Team Sync with Leadership Directs
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Team Meeting - When: Mondays, 11:05-11:45 AM. Stable leadership team, fixed weekly slot. Core anchor meeting for the week.

signals.title: Project Crucible V-Team Weekly Planning
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Meeting - When: Mondays, 8:05-8:30 AM. Small, consistent project v-team. Same project group, fixed weekly rhythm.

signals.title: US Pod Operational Stand-Up
signals.rawExcerpt: US Pod Stand-Up - When: Tuesdays, 12:35-1:00 PM. Stable pod leadership. Recurring operational stand-up with consistent core attendees.

signals.title: Learning and Scaling Fridays Deep-Work Block
signals.rawExcerpt: Learning and Scaling Fridays - PST - When: Fridays, 2:05-5:00 PM. Small fixed group with identical invitees. Long-running weekly block - a structural anchor for the week.

signals.title: CAPE Architecture Weekly Review
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Architecture Call - When: Mondays, 9:05-10:00 AM. CAPE FTE and leadership. Very dense transcribed session covering agent architecture choices tied to eval reliability and customer trust.

signals.title: CAPECAT PM Review Weekly
signals.rawExcerpt: MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review - When: Wednesdays, 9:00-9:30 AM. Core PM review group is steady with broader invite list. Focused on analytics and evaluation metrics.

signals.title: SEVAL Open Office Hours Hosting
signals.rawExcerpt: US / EU and Asia SEVAL Office Hours - When: Tuesdays or Wednesdays, fixed times. SEVAL / BizChat user communities. Same time weekly, open office hours by design. PM serves as primary expert resource for eval guidance.

signals.title: Camp AIR Daily Standup Facilitation
signals.rawExcerpt: Camp AIR Standups - When: Mon-Thu, 1:30-2:00 PM (during March window). Same cohort throughout the program. Extremely consistent while the program is active.

signals.title: Post-Meeting Notes and Action Item Extraction
signals.rawExcerpt: Meeting -> Notes -> Actions workflow: Attend or facilitate a meeting, capture notes in Loop, extract decisions, open questions, and action items. Multiple Loop files explicitly structured with Decisions, Open questions, and Agenda sections. Cognitively expensive but structurally predictable - prime automation candidate.

signals.title: Evals Methodology Advisory (Repeated Expert Consultations)
signals.rawExcerpt: Agent evaluation is by far the most common Teams theme. People repeatedly ask: how to get started with evals, how evals fit into agent development stages, how to handle multi-turn evals, test sets, rubrics, and scoring, why eval runs are slow. PM is seen as the go-to authority for eval methodology.

signals.title: Skills / WorkIQ / Skilluminator Working Chat
signals.rawExcerpt: Small working chats on Skills / WorkIQ: High-volume, hands-on collaboration around Skilluminator, skill detectors, dashboards, bugs, and demos. This is one of the densest clusters of messages. Covers skill detectors/creators, WorkIQ scans and dashboards, bugs, demos, imports, and YAML/MD artifacts.

signals.title: Customer Field Enablement Routing and Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: Customer / field enablement threads - active coordination with field and partner stakeholders around eval enablement, meetings, SuccessHub routing, and external-facing guidance. PM acts as connector: routing feature requests, setting up recurring calls, translating customer pain points into eval and tooling needs.

signals.title: Camp AIR Logistics and Materials Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: Camp AIR / CAPE coordination and logistics: Standups, debriefs, room logistics, and schedules. Where materials live (which SharePoint vs which cohort folder). Links to forms, recordings, labs, and decks.

signals.title: Technical Artifact Sharing (Repos, Zips, Links)
signals.rawExcerpt: Sharing technical artifacts, repos, and working links - frequently posted concrete assets to unblock others: GitHub repos, ZIP files, markdown files, Loop pages, and HTML artifacts for Camp AIR and CAPE deliverables.

signals.title: Eval Deck and Materials Distribution Requests
signals.rawExcerpt: People often come back asking: where slides will be shared, whether a deck applies to their audience or scenario, for clarification or expansion after sessions presented. PM is trusted as both the author and interpreter of eval guidance.

signals.title: Eval Results Triage and Guidance Translation
signals.rawExcerpt: Eval Results -> Triage -> Guidance workflow: Review eval outputs or customer feedback, interpret gaps or failures, translate findings into guidance, decks, or follow-up emails. Repeated reasoning pipeline. Candidate for reusable eval advisor skill.

signals.title: Team-Wide Status Update Standardization Gap
signals.rawExcerpt: Status updates and progress reporting: Many individuals send weekly/bi-weekly updates via Teams, email, slides, or Loop - often answering the same questions in different formats. Same content, different shapes - extra cognitive load and rework for authors.

signals.title: Skilluminator HTML Dashboard Iteration
signals.rawExcerpt: Dashboards and Analytical Artifacts (Very frequent): Multiple iterations of Skilluminator dashboards and analytics snapshots. Repeatedly edited and shared, active iteration and review cycles. Files: dashboard.html, dashboard - v2 - demo.html, CAPE_Eval_Dashboard.html.

signals.title: Weekly Status Report Updates (Loop Pages)
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly status reports (Loop pages): Repeatedly involved in reviewing, contributing to, or sharing weekly status reports across CAPE / Compete / BAT contexts. Examples: Weekly status report - CAPE Compete Agent, Weekly status report - BAT LT AMA Agent.

signals.title: Eval Engagement Tracker Maintenance
signals.rawExcerpt: Eval engagement tracking: PM actively co-maintains the CAPE Eval Engagement Tracker, updated as customer engagements evolve. Structure and recent edits strongly suggest ongoing (often weekly) updates.

signals.title: Loop Meeting Notes Capture and Review
signals.rawExcerpt: Meeting Notes and Collaborative Docs: Reviewed and contributed to Loop-based meeting notes, especially PM review and decision-tracking docs. CAPE / Analytics PM review notes with decisions, open questions, and agenda structure.

signals.title: Customer Signals Planning Spreadsheet Review
signals.rawExcerpt: Planning / signals spreadsheets: Regular collaborator or reviewer on MCS Customer Signals for Planning.xlsx. Updated on a planning cadence (weekly to monthly) as new data comes in and priorities shift.

signals.title: Eval Enablement Deck Proliferation and Consolidation Need
signals.rawExcerpt: Evaluation decks, scenarios, and explanations: Multiple versions of eval intro decks, scenario libraries, how to run evals explanations. Repeated calls to consolidate into one deck / one definition / one repo across meetings. Different teams independently solve the same problem.

harvestNotes: Cycle 36 is a SUCCESSFUL harvest - all 15 WorkIQ queries returned substantive data covering 208-215 emails, 65 calendar events (44h 50m meeting time), and dense Teams/document activity for the week of 2026-03-16. This replaces the failed Cycle 35 harvest. Total signals extracted: 33, covering email (9), meeting (11), teams (9), and document (6) sources.
